steps.title: Identify the angle relationships
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. All four acute angles are congruent, and all four obtuse angles are congruent. Since an acute and adjacent obtuse angle form a linear pair:
$$\text{obtuse} = 180° - \text{acute}$$
The sum of one acute and three obtuse angles is:
$$\text{acute} + 3(180° - \text{acute}) = \text{acute} + 540° - 3\text{acute} = 540° - 2\text{acute}$$

steps.title: Match the form to find w
steps.content:
Rewrite the sum as $-14x + 1760$. The problem states the sum equals $-14x + w$, so:
$$-14x + 1760 = -14x + w$$
Therefore, $w = 1760$.
